comments.name: Maria Petrova
comments.body: Great first post!

comments.name: John Smith
comments.body: Looking forward to more content.

comments.name: Alex Ivanov
comments.body: Very helpful tips, thanks!

comments.name: Anna Kuznetsova
comments.body: I use json-server all the time, great guide.

comments.name: David Brown
comments.body: Perfect for beginners!

comments.name: Olga Smirnova
comments.body: Clear and concise explanation.

comments.name: Michael Johnson
comments.body: Hooks changed the way I write React.

comments.name: Elena Popova
comments.body: useEffect is so powerful.

comments.name: Daniel Wilson
comments.body: Grid is underrated!

comments.name: Irina Morozova
comments.body: Finally understood Flexbox thanks to this.

comments.name: Chris Taylor
comments.body: TypeScript saves so much debugging time.

comments.name: Natalia Volkova
comments.body: Migrated my project to TS last week!

comments.name: Andrew Lee
comments.body: async/await is a game changer.

comments.name: Svetlana Pavlova
comments.body: Good explanation of Promises.

comments.name: Robert Harris
comments.body: Reusable components are the key to scalability.

comments.name: Ekaterina Sokolova
comments.body: We use this pattern at my company.

comments.name: Mark Lewis
comments.body: Redux is overkill for small apps.

comments.name: Yulia Fedorova
comments.body: I prefer Zustand over both!

comments.name: Steven Walker
comments.body: Testing is often overlooked, glad someone wrote this.

comments.name: Polina Andreeva
comments.body: Jest is my go-to for unit tests.

comments.name: Kevin Young
comments.body: Lazy loading made a huge difference for us.

comments.name: Alina Nikitina
comments.body: Don't forget about code splitting!

comments.name: Brian King
comments.body: Accessibility is so important and often ignored.

comments.name: Victoria Orlova
comments.body: ARIA labels helped us pass our audit.

comments.name: Tom Scott
comments.body: REST is still very relevant in 2024.

comments.name: Alex Ivanov
comments.body: Great overview of REST principles.

comments.name: Maria Petrova
comments.body: Design systems save so much time.

comments.name: John Smith
comments.body: We built our own system and never looked back.

comments.name: Anna Kuznetsova
comments.body: Vite is blazing fast compared to Webpack.

comments.name: David Brown
comments.body: Switched to Vite last month, no regrets.

comments.name: Olga Smirnova
comments.body: CSS animations are so smooth nowadays.

comments.name: Michael Johnson
comments.body: GSAP is great for complex animations.

comments.name: Elena Popova
comments.body: React Hook Form is my favorite for forms.

comments.name: Daniel Wilson
comments.body: Formik vs RHF is always a debate.

comments.name: Irina Morozova
comments.body: XSS prevention is a must-know.

comments.name: Chris Taylor
comments.body: Always sanitize user inputs!

comments.name: Natalia Volkova
comments.body: Vercel makes deployment so easy.

comments.name: Andrew Lee
comments.body: We use Docker for our deployments.

comments.name: Svetlana Pavlova
comments.body: ESLint + Prettier is the perfect combo.

comments.name: Robert Harris
comments.body: Consistent code style is so underrated.

comments.name: Ekaterina Sokolova
comments.body: Turborepo changed how we manage our monorepo.

comments.name: Mark Lewis
comments.body: Nx is also a great tool for monorepos.

comments.name: Yulia Fedorova
comments.body: Next.js SSR is amazing for SEO.

comments.name: Steven Walker
comments.body: Nuxt does the same for Vue!

comments.name: Polina Andreeva
comments.body: PWAs feel like native apps on mobile.

comments.name: Kevin Young
comments.body: Service workers are the backbone of PWAs.

comments.name: Alina Nikitina
comments.body: i18n is always tricky to set up but worth it.

comments.name: Brian King
comments.body: We use react-intl and it works great.

comments.name: Victoria Orlova
comments.body: Great series, learned a lot!

comments.name: Tom Scott
comments.body: Looking forward to the next series!
